test: Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct (base, no LoRA) vs v2 LoRA
purpose: Isolate the GRPO+LoRA training contribution: same base model, same params; the only intervention is our reward-engineered GRPO post-training. A statistically significant FPR drop here rules out 'the base model was already that good' as an explanation.
interpretation: Base FPR 16.1% → v2 LoRA FPR 6.7% (delta +9.5 pp, Fisher p = 0.4248). Detection 100.0% → 99.3% (delta -0.7 pp, Fisher p = 1.0000). Same 7B Qwen base, only the GRPO+LoRA training differs — so the FPR drop is attributable to the reward-engineered training.
